Multiple African countries whose ordinary passport holders qualify for VOA at reduced AU rate of NAD 1200: Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cabo Verde, Cameroon, Chad, Comoros, Cote d'Ivoire, Djibouti, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Gabon, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Madagascar, Mauritania, Niger, Rwanda, Sao Tome and Principe, Sierra Leone, Togo, Tunisia, Uganda, Western Sahara Republic. Note: these countries' diplomatic/official/service passport holders also get full visa exemption (see conditional_access). Central African Republic ordinary passport holders were added to VOA list in September 2025 (see separate entry above).

e_visa.notes: E-visa available online via eservices.mhaiss.gov.na for all VOA-eligible nationalities; strongly recommended to apply in advance as it avoids additional NAD 400 manual service fee charged for visa applications submitted on arrival. Holiday visa (up to 3 months) available online.

visa_policy.notes: Effective April 1 2025, Namibia implemented new visa on arrival requirements for nationals of countries that have not reciprocated Namibia's visa exemptions. VOA fee: NAD 1600 for non-AU nationals; NAD 1200 for AU nationals. An additional NAD 400 manual service fee applies for applications submitted on arrival rather than online. E-visa can be applied in advance at eservices.mhaiss.gov.na. Visa-free entry (all passport types) allows up to 3 months per calendar year for tourism/holiday. Diplomatic and official/service passport holders from a wider list of countries are exempted. All visitors must have passport valid 6+ months, return ticket, and at least 3 blank pages. In September 2025, Cabinet approved an additional 35 countries for VOA eligibility (effective approximately September 25, 2025), covering mainly small island states, Caribbean nations, and additional European and Asian countries.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship-by-Investment program exists in Namibia. The Namibian Citizenship Act 14 of 1990 does not provide for citizenship by investment. Citizenship is acquired by birth, descent, or naturalization (after lawful residence). No official government source was found announcing a CBI program.

programs.name: Investment Permit (Employment Permit for Foreign Investors)
programs.notes: Under the Foreign Investments Act 27 of 1990, a foreign national must invest foreign assets of more than N$3,950,000 into a Namibian business activity (or acquire >10% of shares in a Namibian company). This qualifies the investor for a 5-year renewable Employment/Investment Permit. After 7 continuous years with the investment retained, the investor may qualify for Permanent Residence Permit. Applications processed by the Immigration Selection Board (ISB) and Ministry of Home Affairs, Immigration, Safety and Security. Processing time approximately 8 weeks. Note: N$3,950,000 investment threshold is cited by private developers referencing the Foreign Investments Act; the mha.gov.na page does not state the amount directly.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa
programs.administrator: Namibia Investment Promotion and Development Board (NIPDB)
programs.notes: Introduced October 2022. Allows remote workers, freelancers, and self-employed professionals to live and work remotely in Namibia for 6 months (non-renewable; must wait 12 months before reapplying). Income requirement: min USD 2,000/month for applicant; USD 1,000/month for accompanying spouse; USD 500/month per child. Fee: NAD 3,300 payable upon approval. Cannot seek or take up local employment. Administered by NIPDB; applications via the Ministry of Home Affairs portal.

agreements: SADC (Southern African Development Community) — member state; no formal SADC Protocol on Free Movement of Persons in force as of data collection

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1 — Tajikistan reclassified: Moved from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. The previous record noted ambiguity ('may reflect updated policy'); the authoritative April 2025 Swiss Mission list (missionofnamibia.ch) places Tajikistan firmly in the VOA list, and multiple independent post-April-2025 sources confirm VOA status. The Embassy USA source listing it as visa-free predates the April 1 2025 policy change.

CORRECTION 2 — Uzbekistan reclassified: Moved from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. Same reasoning as Tajikistan — the April 1 2025 policy change placed Uzbekistan on the VOA list. Confirmed by the Swiss Mission source and multiple independent references post-April 2025.

CORRECTION 3 — Eswatini source updated: The source_url for Eswatini was previously namibia-botschaft.de (which lists it as 'Swaziland'); updated primary source to visitnamibia.com.na which uses the current name 'Eswatini'.

CORRECTION 4 — September 2025 VOA expansion added: 35 countries added by Cabinet at its 19th session on September 2, 2025 (effective approximately September 25, 2025). New VOA countries: Andorra, Anguilla, Antigua and Barbuda, Aruba, Bahamas, Barbados, Bermuda, Bolivia, Brunei Darussalam, Central African Republic (ordinary passports), Cyprus, Dominican Republic, Georgia, Grenada, Haiti, Kiribati, Maldives, Malta, Monaco, Mongolia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Palestine, Panama, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, Slovenia, Suriname, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Vanuatu. Primary source: The Namibian newspaper (namibian.com.na) — the mha.gov.na pages were inaccessible during this validation.

CORRECTION 5 — Central African Republic dual classification: CAR ordinary passport holders added to VOA list (September 2025 expansion); conditional_access entry retained for CAR diplomatic/official/service passports as those still hold full visa exemption.

CORRECTION 6 — Digital Nomad Visa added to fast_track: This official program (introduced October 2022, administered by NIPDB) was absent from the previous record's fast_track section. Added with official NIPDB source URL. Fee NAD 3,300; min income USD 2,000/month; valid 6 months non-renewable.

CORRECTION 7 — NAD 400 manual service fee noted: An additional NAD 400 service fee is charged for visa applications submitted manually upon arrival vs online via eservices portal. Added to USA entry notes and e_visa section.

CORRECTION 8 — visitnamibia.com.na added to source_urls: This Namibia Tourism Board official source was checked and confirmed the visa-free country list (22 nations excluding Tajikistan/Uzbekistan, confirming reclassification).

CORRECTION 9 — Venezuela reclassification note removed: The prior record's notes on Venezuela's uncertain status have been resolved; Venezuela is confirmed VOA for ordinary passports and visa-free only for diplomatic/official/service passport holders.
CORRECTION 10 — nipdb.com added to official_domains: The Namibia Investment Promotion and Development Board domain hosts the Digital Nomad Visa program information.
gaps: The official MHAISS pages at mha.gov.na (countries-exempted, immigration-control, visas-immigration-control-act-act-no-7) all timed out during this validation session and could not be directly verified; data cross-verified from Embassy USA, Embassy Berlin, Swiss Mission, Visit Namibia, and The Namibian newspaper.
The Namibia Visa Fact Sheet PDF (Version 4, May 2025) from mha.gov.na was not re-fetched in this session; it was previously found to contain only images and no extractable text.
The eservices.mhaiss.gov.na/visaonarrival portal was not directly accessible; complete current VOA country list from the e-services portal not directly verified.

The September 2025 expansion countries (35 new nations) are sourced from The Namibian newspaper, not from mha.gov.na directly. The official effective implementation date was approximately September 25, 2025 per secondary reporting; not confirmed from the official government portal.
No official digital nomad or skilled worker fast-track extension or renewal pathway was found; the Digital Nomad Visa is explicitly non-renewable (must wait 12 months).
Dual citizenship policy under Namibian Citizenship Act not explicitly confirmed for naturalized citizens from official source — not recorded.

The RBI investment amount (N$3,950,000) is cited by private developers referencing the Foreign Investments Act; the official mha.gov.na immigration-control page did not load to confirm the amount directly.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS (type A — held third-country credential): No official Namibian government source found granting visa-free, VOA, e-visa, or eTA entry on the basis of holding a valid US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, Green Card, or any other third-country credential. All entry rules reviewed are purely nationality/passport-type based.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS (type B — special status / OCI): No official Namibian government source found granting entry waiver for OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholders or any equivalent diaspora/heritage status.

Some pre-April-2025 VOA countries sourced only from namibia-botschaft.de (Embassy Berlin) — e.g., Cambodia, Chile, Czech Republic, Hungary, Mexico, Nicaragua, Romania, Slovakia, South Korea, Thailand, UAE, Vietnam — have not been directly re-verified against the eservices portal due to access issues; embassy source treated as authoritative.

visa_free.name: Brazil
visa_free.note: Tourist visits only; business/official trips require visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Rwanda
visa_on_arrival.note: Entry visas issued at any entry point

outbound_official.notes: Source is the Embassy of the Republic of Namibia in Washington DC (namibiaembassyusa.org), an official Namibia government mission site, under the section 'VISA REQUIREMENTS FOR NAMIBIANS TRAVELING ABROAD'. Entries restricted to diplomatic/official/service passports only (Congo Brazzaville, Ethiopia, France, Germany, Ghana, India, Philippines, Switzerland, Turkey, Venezuela) are excluded from the ordinary-passport visa_free list. The mirco.gov.na embassy subdomains (Geneva, Berlin, Kuala Lumpur, Addis Ababa, London, Washington) all publish equivalent 'Namibian Nationals' pages but were unreachable (timeout/ECONNREFUSED) at time of research. No single domestic Namibia government page (mha.gov.na, mirco.gov.na homepage) dedicated solely to outbound travel for Namibia citizens was located; the Embassy USA page is the best available official origin-side source.
